j!Z"e
j#Z$d
Python 3.X compatibility tools.
While this file was originally intended for Python 2 -> 3 transition,
it is now used to create a compatibility layer between different
minor versions of Python 3.
While the active version of numpy may not support a given version of python, we
allow downstream libraries to continue to use these shims for forward
compatibility with numpy while they transition their code to newer versions of
Python.
bytes
asbytes
	isfileobj
getexception
strchar

	asunicode

	os_fspath

isinstancer

7/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/numpy/compat/py3k.pyr
encoder
FileIO

encoding)

filenamer+
exc_infor!
__iter__c
<listcomp>E
z"asbytes_nested.<locals>.<listcomp>)
hasattrr
z$asunicode_nested.<locals>.<listcomp>)
    Check whether obj is a `pathlib.Path` object.
    Prefer using ``isinstance(obj, os.PathLike)`` instead of this function.
    )
objr!
Context manager that does no additional processing.
    Used as a stand-in for a normal context manager, when a particular
    block of code is only sometimes used with a normal context manager:
    cm = optional_cm if condition else nullcontext()
    with cm:
        # Perform operation, using optional_cm if condition is True
    .. note::
        Prefer using `contextlib.nullcontext` instead of this context manager.
    Nc
enter_result)
selfr>
__init__f
contextlib_nullcontext.__init__c
	__enter__i
z contextlib_nullcontext.__enter__c
excinfor!
__exit__l
contextlib_nullcontext.__exit__)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__
__doc__r@
    Load a module.
    .. versionadded:: 1.11.2
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Full module name.
    fn : str
        Path to module file.
    info : tuple, optional
        Only here for backward compatibility with Python 2.*.
    Returns
    -------
    mod : module
    r
SourceFileLoader)
importlib.machineryrH
